title: Honey-Sweet Pooh Bear Day!
story: Every January 18, fans around the world celebrate Winnie the Pooh Day by honoring the beloved honey-loving bear and his friends from the Hundred Acre Wood. People read their favorite Pooh stories, watch classic Disney movies, and share quotes about friendship and simple joys. Many families have Pooh-themed tea parties with honey treats, while libraries host special story times featuring Christopher Robin's adventures. Social media fills with pictures of Pooh merchandise, honey recipes, and childhood memories. Some people even visit real locations that inspired A.A. Milne's stories, like the original Poohsticks Bridge in England, making this day a sweet celebration of timeless friendship and imagination.
activity_question: What do many families do to celebrate Winnie the Pooh Day?
activity_choices: Have Pooh-themed tea parties with honey treats
Build tree houses in their backyards
Dress up like bees and buzz around
activity_answer: Have Pooh-themed tea parties with honey treats
bonus_fact: The real Winnie was actually a female black bear who lived at the London Zoo!

title: Words, Words Everywhere on Thesaurus Day!
story: Every January 18, word lovers celebrate National Thesaurus Day by hunting for bigger, better, and more exciting ways to say things! Instead of saying "good," people try "fantastic," "marvelous," or "stupendous." Teachers challenge students to rewrite sentences using fancier words, while writers dig through their thesauruses to make their stories pop with amazing vocabulary that sounds way cooler than boring old words.
activity_question: What do people use a thesaurus to find?
activity_choices: Different ways to say the same thing
activity_answer: Different ways to say the same thing
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting word you could use instead of 'happy'?

title: Duck Into Something Delicious!
story: Crispy skin crackles as chefs slice perfectly roasted Peking duck on January 18! This National Peking Duck Day celebrates one of China's most famous dishes. Restaurants across America serve this special meal with thin pancakes, sweet sauce, and fresh scallions. Families gather to wrap tender duck meat in the soft pancakes, creating delicious little bundles. Food lovers post photos of their fancy duck dinners online, showing off the golden, crispy skin that makes this dish so special.
activity_question: What do people wrap Peking duck in when they eat it?
bonus_fact: Traditional Peking duck takes over 24 hours to prepare properly!
